content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
James D. Thomas 
5232 Calle Cristobal 
Santa Barbara, CA 
93111 
Dear Mr. Thomas: 
July 6, 1989 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-89-356 
You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided 
to you concerning the campaign provisio~ of the Political Reform 
Act. 11 
You have requested this advice on behalf of Santa Barbara 
Councilmember Jeanne Graffy. 
Your letter correctly summarizes my advice that Councilmember 
Graffy may use campaign funds received prior to January 1, 1989, 
for officeholder expenses. 
In addition, if the funds were 
received in compliance with the contribution limitations of 
Proposition 73 they may also be used for her mayoral race in the 
November 1989 election. 
It is also correct that if Councilmember 
Graffy spends these funds for officeholder and campaign related 
expenses and this interpretation of Proposition 73 changes, she 
probably will not be required to reimburse the funds. 
Enclosed 
please find Regulation 18536.221 which discusses use of funds 
received prior to January 1, 1989, and the Leidioh Advice Letter, 
No. A-89-100, which discusses reimbursement of funds. 
A copy of 
your letter is also enclosed for your convenience. 
I/Government Code sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory references 
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission 
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 
18000, et seq. 
All references to regulations are to Title 2, 
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 
21 
Regulation 18536.2 was adopted as an emergency regulation on 
May 2, 1989. 
The regulation was disapproved by the Office of 
Administrative Law, and an appeal to the governor is pending. 
In 
the meantime, we are advising based on that regulation, since it 
represents the most recent expression of Commission policy. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • 
(916)322~5660 


File No. A-89-356 
Page 2 
Please call me at (916) 322-5662 if you have additional ques-
tions. 
Enclosures 
Sincerely, 
Kathryn E. Donovan 
General Counsel 
, 
'Xl 
(!; rrrit)-./n1 fLutl{U{/ 
By: 
Linda Moureaux 
Political Reform Consultant 


June 6, 1989 
Linda Moureaux 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
428 J Street 
Sacramento, CA 95814 
Dear Ms Moureaux, 
As a representative of the Jeanne Graffy for Mayor of Santo 
Barbaro Committee, tllis letter is to confirm our telephone 
conversation yesterday at 4:30 P,M, 
, , 
As I said on the phone, Mrs. Graffy has approximately $10,000 
left in her political account from her successful election to 
City Council lost year. 
Our previous understanding of 
Propositions 68 and 73 was that we would not be allowed to 
use that money for anything except current officeholder 
expenses. 
Based on our conversation, this is to confirm that the money 
left over from her previous election to City Council can now 
be used for officeholder expenses and for her campaign for 
Mayor in the November 1989 election. 
We understand that the 
money in Question would have to still meet the current 
restrictions with regards to maximum contribution amounts 
based on the fiscal year. 
It is also our understanding that 
if Mrs. Graffy spends money from the account in Question and 
the interpretations of the propositions change that she is 
not required to reimburse her previous account. 
Please respond in writing for our records if the above 
information is correct or send information which would change 
our understanding of the current situation. 
Please send to: 
James D. Thomas 
5232 Cal]e Cristobal 
Santo Barbara, CA 93111 
Thank you for your courtesy on the telephone and for your 
cooperation in sending us a confirmation letter. 
C 
ttee 




California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
James D. Thomas 
Campaign Coordinator 
June 14, 1989 
Jeanne Graffy for Mayor Committee 
5232 Calle Cristobal 
Santa Barbara, CA 
93111 
Re: 
Letter No. 89-356 
Dear Mr. Thomas: 
We received your letter requesting confirmation of advice 
under the Political Reform Act on June 8, 1989. 
Your letter has 
been assigned to our Technical Assistance and Analysis Division 
for response. 
If you have any questions, you may contact that 
division directly at (916) 322-5662. 
If the letter is appropriate for confirmation without further 
analysis, we will attempt to expedite our response. 
A confirming 
response will be released after it has gone through our approval 
process. 
If the letter is not appropriate for this treatment, the 
staff person assigned to prepare the response will contact you 
shortly to advise you. 
In such cases, the normal analysis, review 
and approval process will be followed. 
You should be aware that your letter and our response are 
public records which may be disclosed to any interested person 
upon receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
KED:plh:confadvl 
Sincerely, 
Kathryn E. Donovan 
General Counsel 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • 
(916)322~5660

sections.question: Whether campaign funds received prior to January 1, 1989 may be used for officeholder expenses and for a mayoral campaign in the November 1989 election, whether such funds must comply with Proposition 73 contribution limitations, and whether reimbursement would be required if the interpretation of the propositions changes.
sections.conclusion: Councilmember Graffy may use campaign funds received prior to January 1, 1989, for officeholder expenses. In addition, if the funds were received in compliance with the contribution limitations of Proposition 73 they may also be used for her mayoral race in the November 1989 election. If Councilmember Graffy spends these funds for officeholder and campaign related expenses and this interpretation of Proposition 73 changes, she probably will not be required to reimburse the funds.

sections.question_synthetic: May a Santa Barbara councilmember use approximately $10,000 in leftover campaign funds from her previous city council election for both current officeholder expenses and her 1989 mayoral campaign, and must these funds comply with Proposition 73 contribution limits?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: FPPC confirms that pre-January 1, 1989 campaign funds may be used for both officeholder expenses and the 1989 mayoral campaign if they comply with Proposition 73 contribution limitations. The requestor will likely not be required to reimburse such funds if the legal interpretation changes in the future.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: Document contains both the incoming request (dated June 6, 1989) and FPPC's formal response advice letter (dated July 6, 1989), plus an acknowledgment letter (dated June 14, 1989). The main advice letter is dated July 6, 1989 and signed by Linda Moureaux, Political Reform Consultant, on behalf of Kathryn E. Donovan, General Counsel. File number A-89-356 matches document ID 89-356.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether campaign funds received prior to January 1, 1989 may be used for officeholder expenses and for a mayoral campaign in the November 1989 election, whether such funds must comply with Proposition 73 contribution limitations, and whether reimbursement would be required if the interpretation of the propositions changes.

CONCLUSION: Councilmember Graffy may use campaign funds received prior to January 1, 1989, for officeholder expenses. In addition, if the funds were received in compliance with the contribution limitations of Proposition 73 they may also be used for her mayoral race in the November 1989 election. If Councilmember Graffy spends these funds for officeholder and campaign related expenses and this interpretation of Proposition 73 changes, she probably will not be required to reimburse the funds.

embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter confirming telephone counsel regarding Santa Barbara Councilmember Jeanne Graffy's use of approximately $10,000 in leftover campaign funds from her city council election for both officeholder expenses and her 1989 mayoral campaign, subject to Proposition 73 contribution limitations.
